observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

   1. Instruct the Local Finance Committee to strictly implement a  plan  on
      allocating the available cash and come up with  possible  measures  to
      avoid further incurrence of  cash  overdraft  while  intensifying  the
      LGU’s collections to increase its current cash balance.

   2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor  and  ensure  that  cash  is
      properly allocated upon certification of cash availability to avoid  a
      deficit.
   3. Require each department head to implement projects  as  scheduled  and
      refrain from  incurring  unexpected  expenses  that  may  deplete  the
      available      cash      balance      intended       for       planned
      projects/programs/activities.

   4. Ensure that any overdraft which may be incurred at the end of the year
      in any local fund by virtue of the provisions hereof shall be  covered
      with the first collections of the immediately succeeding  fiscal  year
      accruing to such local fund as mandated in Section 337 of RA 7160.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the LGU shall take advantage of  the  latest  technology
by adopting the system of  payroll  payment  through  the  automated  teller
machine of Development  Bank  of  the  Philippines  (DBP)-Mati  City,  Davao
Oriental, its depository bank, in order to strengthen the  control  on  cash
management.

In addition, the management is encouraged to explore all  options  in  order
to boost the internet connection and follow up with its depository  bank  on
the status of their request in order to expedite the installation of ATM.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following:

     1.  For  the  Municipal  Accountant,  prepare  and  maintain  all  the
        corresponding PPE ledger cards for all recorded PPE in  the  books.
        If warranted, expedite the inclusion of the PPE ledger cards in the
        accounting software of the LGU.

     2. For the OIC Municipal Treasurer, prepare and maintain the PPE cards
        following the prescribed format.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

     1. Request the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate  budget  and  allocate
        the necessary funds for the payment of processing fees that may  be
        incurred in the titling thereof.


     2. Direct the GSO-Designate or the personnel-in-charge to expedite the
        transfer of ownership of the aforesaid parcels of land in the  name
        of the LGU to protect the interest of the government.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

   1. Instruct the GSO-Designate to prepare and accomplish the corresponding
      Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP)  for
      the identified unserviceable properties and initiate  the  process  of
      disposing thereof in accordance with Section 379 of RA 7160; and

   2. Require the Municipal Accountant to drop the unserviceable  properties
      from the books of accounts in accordance with the existing  accounting
      and auditing rules and regulations after submission of  the  documents
      by the GSO-Designate pertinent to the disposal  of  the  unserviceable
      properties.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

     1. Create an Inventory Committee who shall be temporarily relieved  of
        all their regular duties to devote their full time to  the  conduct
        of the physical inventory taking until the  same  is  completed  in
        three (3) months or less as required under Sections 5.2 and 5.3  of
        the said COA Circular.


     2. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan
        (PIP), have it approved by the  Municipal  Mayor,  and  submit  the
        approved PIP to the Audit Team at  least  ten  (10)  calendar  days
        before the scheduled start of the inventory taking activities.


     3. Ensure the full implementation of the “One-time  cleansing  of  PPE
        accounts” and undertake all other procedures as provided for in COA
        Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and  correct  balances
        of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendations:

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

     1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO-Designate  to  comply  with  the
        prescribed guidelines set under COA Circular No. 2018-002 in  order
        to insure the properties and supplies of the LGU and to conform  to
        RA No. 656, as amended by PD No. 245; and


     2.  Request the Sangguniang Bayan to provide a supplemental budget for
        the application of insurance for the properties.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


   1. As head of the agency, to ensure the optimal utilization  of  the  20%
      development fund in accordance with the revised guidelines  to  attain
      the desired outcomes for the benefit of the constituents of Tarragona.



   2. Direct the department heads concerned to prioritize the implementation
      of the remaining 6 projects  during  the  1st  quarter  of  the  year,
      likewise, the proposed developmental projects intended for CY 2022.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Management shall ensure optimal utilization  of  the
available MDRRMF for disaster risk management  activities  and  purchase  of
disaster rescue and response equipment to  ensure  readiness  in  mitigating
the effect of any disaster. Moreover, MDRRMC shall incorporate  and  program
in the LDRRMFIP the unexpended balance of QRF  and  DRRMF-MOOE  of  previous
years  in  the  current  LDRRMFIP.  Lastly,  the  Municipal  Accountant  and
Municipal Budget Officer shall reconcile the noted discrepancy.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following to the Management:


        1. The release and  use  of  QRF  shall  be  supported  with  an  SB
           Resolution declaring the locality under  the  state  of  calamity
           pertaining to  a  specific  disaster,  calamity,  insurgency  and
           catastrophe as mandated in NDDRMC, DMB and  DILG  Joint  Circular
           No. 2013-1;


        2. Utilize the fund according to its purpose;


        3.  Submit  the  correct  and  proper  documentation  for  the  fuel
           consumption of the aforesaid transaction.

Recommendations:


We recommend that the MDRRMO shall prepare the  monthly  Report  of  Sources
and Utilization of MDRRMF to be certified by the  Municipal  Accountant  and
submit the same to the Office of the Auditor  on  or  before  the  15th  day
after the end of  each  month  through  the  LDRRMC  and  Local  Development
Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor of the LGU. If necessary, there shall be  a
personnel under the direct supervision of the MDRRM Officer to  be  assigned
with the preparation and submission of the said report.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Management coordinate with DA-BFAR and  comply  with
the Memorandum of Agreement  mentioned  above  so  that  the  CFLC  will  be
utilized in accordance with the purpose for which it was established.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
observe the deadline set in Section 9  of  RA  5447  on  the  remittance  of
provincial shares accruing to the SEF so that the Provincial Government  can
timely utilize the collections to finance various  projects,  programs,  and
activities  beneficial  to  the  constituents  of the  province  of   Davao
Oriental.

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall monitor  and  remind  regularly  the
staff in-charge of the timely remittance of  the  RPT  share  to  avoid  the
incurrence of delay.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

     1.  Require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  exercise  her  review  and
        monitoring functions over the collections turned over to her by the
        Liquidating Officer and/or  collectors  and  deposit  intact  those
        collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not  later
        than the next banking day as prescribed in Section 32, Chapter 3 of
        the NGAS Manual.


     2. Institute stricter policies on cash management and ensure  controls
        are in place to prevent  possible  risks  of  misappropriations  or
        losses due to theft or fraud.

We recommended that management enforce the  immediate  liquidation  of
the outstanding cash advances through the issuance of letters of  demand  to
officials and employees concerned, and strictly comply with  the  provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and  liquidation  of
cash advances.


      Also, conduct regular and periodic verification and  analysis  of  the
existence of receivables and unliquidated cash  advances  to  determine  the
extent of collectability. Management is,  likewise,  encouraged  to  request
write-off on  dormant  receivable  accounts  by  submitting  duly  certified
relevant documents.

We reiterate our previous recommendations that the inventory committee
ensures the  conduct  of  physical  count  of  all  the  properties  of  the
municipality. Thereafter, prepare the report with the corresponding  remarks
on the condition of  the  listed  equipment  and  reconcile  the  same  with
accounting records and take appropriate action on any  determined  variance.
Submit a report on the result of reconciliation which  shall  be  our  basis
for the appropriate opinion and further action.

We recommended that Management:

     1. Require the Municipal Accountant and the  responsible  offices,  as
        mentioned in Table 1, to submit immediately  the  reported  lacking
        vouchers, financial records and other reports within the prescribed
        period to effect timely review and evaluation of  the  transactions
        as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy  and  to  preclude
        the issuance of Notices of Suspension on these transactions.


     2. Adopt measures  that  would  ensure  the  submission  of  financial
        accounts and reports within the  prescribed  period.   And  in  the
        event of  repeated  and  unjustified  failure  of  the  accountable
        officers concerned to submit these accounts and reports, impose the
        applicable sanctions as provided in Section 122  (2)  of  PD  1445,
        which states:


           “Failure on the part of the officials concerned  to  submit  the
           documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause
           the suspension of payment of their  salaries  until  they  shall
           have  complied  with  the  requirements  of   the   Commission.”
           (Emphasis ours)

We recommended that management shall enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Also, require Ms. Lague to render account on the long outstanding cash advance pertaining to MSAP and cause the return of any excess of the two-month budgetary requirement to reduce the risk of loss or misuse.
Moreover, conduct of regular and periodic verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent of collectability.
A request for the write-off shall be made on dormant receivable accounts by submitting duly certified relevant documents.

We recommended that management shall strengthen the workforce of the MDRRM office by appropriating the funds needed for the mandatory positions. If the financial capability of the agency permits, hire additional personnel other than those required by the JMC to ensure proper functioning of the office in the implementation and monitoring of PPAs, thereby reduce disaster risks and life-threatening damages to the entire populace of Tarragona.
Also, submit the required reports per established laws, rules and regulations.

None

Management submitted such reports

The management could not appropriate
fund to hire additional personnel due
to budgetary constraints but the management
would adopt measures to compensate the
lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Ensure that succeeding transfers to barangays shall be for purposes aligned with the updated guidelines on the utilization of the 20% development fund. Also, POWs for infrastructure projects and programmed expenditures shall attach as supporting documents and shall become the basis for the amount transferred.
To facilitate the monitoring of implementation of such transfers, MOA shall be executed between the involved parties. Further, all transactions shall be recorded as Due from Local Government Units to monitor implementation.
Strictly comply with the documentary requirements on the transfer of funds to implementing agencies pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001.
Ensure that fund transfers are properly taken up in the books of both source agency and implementing agency.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2017
Management complied with the audit recommendation.

Management complied with the audit recommendation.

Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 adopting public bidding as the general mode of procurement. For this purpose, the agency shall see to it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for such public bidding. Alternative methods should be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in Rule XVI of IRR-A of RA 9184. Likewise, emergency purchase shall only be resorted to if the circumstances warrant the same. Moreover, ensure that supply requirements are meticulously and judiciously programmed for the whole year which shall be contained in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). No government procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan of the Procuring Entity.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2017
None
observations.reason:
Alternative mode of procurement is
still being used for procurement which could be
done through public bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We require immediate submission of the aforementioned reports and documents to allow timely evaluation, analysis, and audit of accounts and transactions.
Also, management is reminded that pursuant to Sec. 39 (3) of PD 1445, non-submission of pertinent reports without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for administrative disciplinary action as well as for disallowing permanently a claim under examination.

For this purpose, the agency shall see to it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for such public bidding. Alternative methods should be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in Rule XVI of IRR-A of RA 9184. Likewise, emergency purchase shall only be resorted to if the circumstances warrant the same. Moreover, ensure that supply requirements are meticulously and judiciously programmed for the whole year which shall be contained in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). No government procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan of the Procuring Entity.

AAR CY 2017
Management complied with the audit recommendation by submitting the vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We require the preparation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and the Annual Work and Financial Plan which should be compliant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73. The utilization of 70% of the LDRRMF shall be in accordance with said plan.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2017
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and the Annual Work and Financial Plan were prepared.
observations.reason: Prior years’ unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE was not included.

observations.recommendation:
Adhere strictly to the above-quoted law and other applicable existing rules and regulations to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of plans, programs and priorities of the LGU. Likewise, adopt a policy and relevant control measures to ensure faithful observance and compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations to, at least, minimize the incurrence of expenditures deemed to be disadvantageous to the government.
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to implement competitive bidding as a mode of procurement in all procurement actions except in cases when alternative modes of procurement of R.A. 9184 may apply.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers before processing and paying the claims of creditor/suppliers. Reimbursements should be limited to cases where it is impractical to resort to regular mode of procurement and payment.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2017
The BAC was directed to implement competitive bidding as a mode of procurement in all procurement actions except in cases when alternative modes of procurement of R.A. 9184 may apply.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers before processing and paying the claims of creditor/suppliers.
Reimbursements should be limited to cases where it is impractical to resort to regular mode of procurement and payment.
observations.reason: Management still resorted to procurement of supplies thru reimbursement.

observations.recommendation:
Stop the payment of extra benefits to officials and employees in form of incentives and financial assistance from the Trust Fund without legal basis and authority.
Settle the disallowances that will be issued in the disbursements of funds for this purpose.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2017
The Municipal Accountant imposed payroll deduction in settlement of issued Notices of Disallowance.
observations.reason: There are 5 employees who are no longer connected to the LGU hence preventing any action that may be imposed thereon in settlement of the disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
Adhere strictly on the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances provided for under Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular no. 97-002, COA Circular No. 2003-003, Section 174 of the GAAM Vol. I and other pertinent rules and regulations.
Limit the designation or assignment of special disbursing officer to the number prescribed under existing rules and regulations on the granting of cash advances.
Demand the immediate settlement of outstanding cash advances which had remained outstanding as of the year-end.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2017
The Municipal Accountant has sent Demand Letters during the 1st and 2nd semester of the year and has required concerned personnel to liquidate previous cash advances before granting another cash advance.
observations.reason: Several cash advances still remain outstanding and have remained dormant for several years.

observations.recommendation: Submit a written justification on the deficiencies noted in the procurement of the generator set. Impose the payment of liquidated damages.
observations.reason: No evidence of the issued receipt of payment for liquidated damages was submitted to this office.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that for subsequent transactions, the amount of the warranty security posted by the contractor is in accordance with the rates prescribed in Section 62.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: No explanation was sent by management.

observations.recommendation: Align the plan with the PPAs identified in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1; Allocate funds from the General Fund for the regular repair and maintenance activities of the municipality and payment of loan amortization for the completion of office building; Justify the deficiencies noted in the procurement of fuel; Prepare the necessary journal entries to record assets previously recognized as expense; and Submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and the necessary lacking documents to avoid suspension/disallowance.
observations.reason: The same observation was made in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Submit a written justification on the failure to publish the ITB in a newspaper of nationwide circulation and non-invitation of observers on all stages of the procurement.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 indicated: Management submitted a certificate of publication dated February 12, 2016 from Abrigana News with office address at Capitol Hill, Barangay Central, Mati City, Davao Oriental. Attached thereto was a printout of the publication of the Invitation to Bid. A copy of the duly accomplished Purchase Order was also submitted. Likewise, the pertinent bidding documents such as the PR, Abstract of Bids, etc. were forwarded to COA.
observations.reason: Abrigana News is not considered a newspaper of nationwide general circulation as prescribed in RA 9184. Moreover, the original copy of the PO attached to the DV was not duly accomplished; hence, the validity of the duplicate copy submitted by BAC which was found to be duly accomplished except for the terms of delivery, is deemed doubtful.

observations.recommendation: Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine extent of collectability. Request for the write-off of dormant accounts and submit the certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.
observations.reason: The same audit observation was made in CY2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: Ensure that fund transfers to implementing agencies are covered with a Memorandum of Agreement requiring the submission of liquidation by the Implementing Agency for proper monitoring of fund utilization. Strictly comply with the documentary requirements on the transfer of funds to implementing agencies pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001. Ensure that fund transfers are properly taken up in the books of both source agency and implementing agency.
observations.managementAction: Management has not yet initiated any action to implemented the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The same audit observation was made in CY2017.

observations.recommendation: Fast track the settlement of accounts suspended amounting to P2,136,460.37 to avoid legal sanctions that maybe imposed. Adhere to Section 7.1.1 and 7.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to require the employees concerned to refund the disallowances amounting to P16,640.00 and submit the required lacking documentation in AOM amounting to P302,094.01.
observations.managementAction: Management plans to fast track settlement of the suspensions and disallowances.
observations.reason: Management is on the process of locating the related transaction documents.

observations.recommendation: Require concerned officials and employees involved in the procurement process to review documents before approval by the higher authorities and strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 by submitting to COA copies of contracts and POs within five (5) days after its issuance or perfection.
observations.reason: POs and Copies of Contracts were still not forwarded to COA.

observations.recommendation: Fast track the settlement of accounts suspended amounting to P1,648,690.16 to avoid legal sanctions that maybe imposed. Adhere to Section 7.1.1 and 7.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the officers concerned to refund the amount of P277,056.30.
observations.reason: Management is on the process of locating the related transaction documents.

observations.recommendation: Secure the records and documents that support the information that the total amount of P2,062,668.42 are really the accountabilities of the Municipal Treasurer Belino Jimenez. If this could be proven, work for the writing off of this account with authority from COA so that this can be cleared from the book of the LGU. As to the recorded accounts of former Municipal Treasurer Evelyn Vergara, enforce the collection of this account from her. Issue letter of demand for her to settle this obligation to the LGU of Tarragona. Moreover, exert effort to identify the recorded miscellaneous receivable of P7,299.31 so that persons accountable thereof should be obliged to settle the same.
observations.managementAction: Demand letter was issued to Ms. Evelyn Vergara for settlement of obligations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant cannot comply with the audit recommendation there being no available documents as basis for further action. There were no documents related to the aforesaid transaction turned over from the previous Accountant.

observations.recommendation: Make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts. Moreover, compute and record the related depreciation expense to reflect the correct PPE book balance at year end.
observations.managementAction: Municipal accountant made the necessary entries on January 31, 2017 per JEV No. 101-0117-001

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the aforesaid lacking documents.

Investment Plan should be prepared indicating therein the specific PPAs to be funded and implemented out of the LDRRMF and integrate the same in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality. Submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day of the succeeding month to the Office of the Auditor. Allocate funds from the General Fund for the administrative expenses of the MDRRM Office and ensure that charges to the LDRRM fund are limited only to PPAs in support of disaster risk reduction and management activities. Justify the deficiencies noted in the procurement of fuel, food commodities and rescue vehicles. Prepare the necessary journal entries to reclassify the disaster equipment and its related accumulated depreciation. Submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and the necessary lacking documents to avoid suspension/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Adjusting entries were made. However, other recommendations were not complied.

observations.recommendation: Advise the Municipal School Board to align programs, projects and activities with the pertinent provisions of DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998 and discontinue appropriating funds for expenses which are not among those prioritized to be charged against SEF to preclude suspensions and/or disallowance in audit. Discontinue the granting of cash advance to non-organic personnel of the municipality and to require immediate liquidation of cash advances granted to them; and Direct the accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to reclassify the advances granted to non-organic personnel.
observations.reason: The verification of the disbursements was hindered due to low utilization of the SEF in CY2017.

observations.recommendation: Direct the inventory/property committee to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment. Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties. Consider PPE that may be included in the undetermined forwarded balance. Submit a report on the result of reconciliation including the final undetermined balance which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action. Maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of actual physical count and the recorded balances in the accounting/property records.
observations.managementAction: On February 13, 2017, the accountant submitted subsidiary ledgers of the PPE with additional columns “Serviceable” and “Unserviceable” and signed by four LGU personnel to support that the reconstituted inventory and disposal committee conducted the physical count.
observations.reason: The submitted report was not in prescribed form. Moreover, the result of the physical count was not reconciled with the PPE balance per accounting records. Pending the result of the conduct of physical count and the reconciliation thereof with available accounting records, the PPE balance at year-end remains doubtful. Despite the reconstitution of the Inventory Committee, same audit observation was made in CY2017.

observations.recommendation: Reiteration of previous recommendation that management should direct the personnel concerned to strictly observe and submit the required financial and other reports within the prescribed deadlines to effect timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy, otherwise, the penal provision under Section 122 of PD 1445 shall be applied.
observations.reason: Some of the lacking documents were submitted to this office.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above observations, we recommended the following: Ensure the reconstitution/creation of the Committee on Inventory and Disposal and direct the committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment. Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties. Submit a report on the result of reconciliation which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action. Maintain Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of physical count and accounting/property records.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive has issued Executive Order No. 18-As. 2016 to reconstitute the Inventory Committee. Actual physical count was conducted on December 2016. Property records were generated by the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The submitted report was not in prescribed form. Moreover, the result of the physical count was not reconciled with the PPE balance per accounting records. Pending the result of the conduct of physical count and the reconciliation thereof with available accounting records, the PPE balance at year-end remains doubtful.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management stop utilizing LDRRMF for purposes other than for which the fund was created. It is also recommended that preparation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and the Annual Work and Financial Plan should be compliant with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and ensure that utilization of the same is in accordance with the plan. Lastly, submit the lacking disbursement vouchers to preclude the issuance of notice of suspension.
observations.reason: The same audit observation was made in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Local School Board to effectively plan for the specific programs, projects and activities which strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of Republic Act No. 7160 and DECS/DBM/DILG Joint Circular no 01 series of 1998 dated April 14, 1998 giving more priority to repair and maintenance of school facilities and equipment and construction of buildings and other school facilities. Fast track implementation of PPAs during the year.
observations.managementAction: The LSB was advised of the deficiencies in the use of SEF with emphasis on alignment of the plan with existing guidelines.
observations.reason: PPAs were not timely implemented in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Render proper accounting of the aforementioned cash advances and submit the lacking documents pursuant to COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation was made on cash advances amounting to P85,000.00
observations.reason: Cash advances amounting to P165,000.00 were not yet liquidated due to lack of reference since DVs were already forwarded to COA.

observations.recommendation: Adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 9184 adopting public bidding as the general mode of procurement. For this purpose, the agency shall see to it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for such public bidding. Alternative methods should be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in Rule XVI of IRR-A of RA 9184. Likewise, ensure that supply requirements are meticulously and judiciously programmed for the whole year which shall be contained in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). No government procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan of the Procuring Entity. Moreover, ensure that supply requirements are meticulously and judiciously programmed for the whole year which shall be contained in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). No government procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan of the Procuring Entity.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was directed to ensure that the agency adopts public bidding as the main mode of procurement. The Municipal Accountant shall ensure completeness of supporting documents.
observations.reason: Management still resorted to procurement of supplies thru reimbursement.

observations.recommendation: We require immediate submission of the aforementioned reports and documents to allow timely evaluation, analysis, and audit of accounts and transactions. Also, management is reminded that pursuant to Sec. 39 (3) of PD 1445, non-submission of pertinent reports without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for administrative disciplinary action as well as for disallowing permanently a claim under examination.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer was directed to forward the Report on Collection and Deposit at the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Submission of transaction documents was still substantially delayed.

observations.reason: Lacking DVs remain unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation:
Adopt the perpetual inventory method in the procurement of office supplies, food supplies, drugs and medicines, medical and dental supplies, gasoline, oil and lubricants, agricultural and other supplies. It is further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer as the case maybe, prepare and maintain the records and reports required by NGAS Manual to ensure that the financial report reflect the correct account balance.

observations.recommendation:
Make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the  correct  balances
of the accounts. Moreover,  compute  and  record  the  related  depreciation
expense to reflect the correct PPE book balance at year end.

a. Submit a written justification on the  deficiencies  noted  in  the
       procurement of the generator set.
     b. Impose the payment of liquidated damages.

Ensure that for subsequent transactions, the amount  of  the  warranty
security  posted  by  the  contractor  is  in  accordance  with  the   rates
prescribed in Section 62.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.
Submit immediately the aforesaid lacking documents.

a. Investment Plan should be prepared indicating therein the  specific
        PPAs to be funded and implemented out of the LDRRMF  and  integrate
        the same in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.
     b. Submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of  LDRRMF  on  or
        before the 15th day of the succeeding month to the  Office  of  the

     c. Allocate  funds  from  the  General  Fund  for  the  administrative
        expenses of the MDRRM Office and ensure that charges to  the  LDRRM
        fund are limited only to PPAs in support of disaster risk reduction
        and management activities.

a. Advise the Municipal School Board to  align  programs,  projects
           and activities with the pertinent  provisions  of  DECS-DBM-DILG
           Joint Circular No. 01  dated  April  14,  1998  and  discontinue
           appropriating funds for expenses which  are  not  among  those
           prioritized to be charged against SEF  to  preclude  suspensions
           and/or disallowance in audit


        b.  Discontinue  the  granting  of  cash  advance  to   non-organic
           personnel  of  the  municipality  and   to   require   immediate
           liquidation of cash advances granted to them; and

        c. Direct the accountant to prepare the necessary  journal  entries

Submit a written justification on the failure to publish the ITB in  a
newspaper of general nationwide circulation and non-invitation of  observers
on all stages of the procurement.

a. Direct the inventory/property committee to submit the Report on the
        Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)  with  the
        corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment.


     b. Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting  records  to

     c. Submit a report on the result of reconciliation including the final
        undetermined balance which  shall  be  our  basis  for  appropriate

     d.  Maintain  PPE  Ledger  Cards  and  Property  Cards  to  facilitate
        reconciliation of actual physical count and the  recorded  balances

a. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash  advances
        thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials  and  employees
        concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of  COA  Circular
        97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

        determine extent of collectability.

        certified  relevant  documents  validating  the  existence  of  the

Reiteration of previous recommendation that management  should  direct
the  personnel  concerned  to  strictly  observe  and  submit  the  required
financial and other  reports  within  the  prescribed  deadlines  to  effect
timely  review  and  evaluation  of  transactions  as  to  their  propriety,
completeness and accuracy, otherwise, the penal provision under Section  122
of PD 1445 shall be applied.

In view of the above observations, we recommended the following: Ensure the reconstitution/creation of the Committee on Inventory and Disposal and direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment.
Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties.
Submit a report on the result of reconciliation which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.
Maintain Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of physical count and accounting/property records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management propose the passage of an ordinance establishing municipal/local scholarship program, specifying, among others, the composition of the Scholarship Board/Committee, the eligibility requirements, qualifying maximum family income level of applicants to ensure that only poor students, as determined under RA 8425, can avail of the scholarship, in addition to other requirements such as minimum grade, expenses to be covered by the scholarship, specific degree courses, obligation of grantees, the circumstances considered as breach and sanctions to be imposed thereon.

2015
Municipal Ordinance No. 05 s. 2012 was approved to establish the local scholarship program.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 adopting public bidding as the general mode of procurement. For this purpose, the agency shall see to it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for such public bidding. Alternative methods should be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in Rule XVI of IRR-A of RA 9184. Likewise, ensure that supply requirements are meticulously and judiciously programmed for the whole year which shall be contained in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). No government procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan of the Procuring Entity.
Moreover, ensure that supply requirements are met meticulously and judiciously programmed for the whole year which shall be contained in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). No government procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan of the Procuring Entity.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
CY
2015
observations.reason: Alternative mode of procurement is still being used.

observations.recommendation: We require immediate submission of the aforementioned reports and documents to allow timely evaluation, analysis, and audit of accounts and transactions. Also, management is reminded that pursuant to Sec. 39 (3) of PD 1445, non-submission of pertinent reports without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for administrative disciplinary action as well as for disallowing permanently a claim under examination.

2015
The Treasury Office was already informed of their responsibility to forward the transaction documents to the Accountant’s Office at the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The Treasury Office was still not able to submit pertinent reports on time.

observations.recommendation: We require immediate submission of the reported lacking vouchers including the unsubmitted vouchers for 2013 and 2014 otherwise a Notice of Suspension will be issued on the transactions for which the concerned Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant could be held jointly and severally liable thereof as prescribed under Section 104 of PD 1445.

2015
Some of the lacking DVs were already forwarded to COA.
observations.reason: Delay in the forwarding of transaction documents by the Treasury Office caused the non-submission of the remaining lacking DVs.

observations.recommendation: Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances in the total amount of P1,422,097.00 thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned. Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

2014
The Municipal Accountant has sent Demand Letters during the 1st and 2nd semester of the year and has required concerned personnel to liquidate previous cash advances before granting another cash advance.
observations.reason: The outstanding balances remain unliquidated despite the issuance of demand letters.

observations.recommendation:
Request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the transfer of these properties to its beneficiaries. Facilitate the transfer of these properties to the recipient schools and LGUs including proper titling thereof in the name of the agency having custody.
Correspondingly, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) for the dropping of these assets from the books of the agency.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
CY
2014
The aforesaid funds were already returned to the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: The submitted funds were already returned to the Trust Fund. The Municipal Accountant cannot comply with the audit recommendatio n there being no available documents as basis for further action. There were no documents related to the aforesaid transaction turned over from the previous Accountant.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management stop the practice of transferring Trust Funds to the General Fund and utilizing said funds for undertakings that are not consistent with its main purpose.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
CY
2014
Management has initiated the strengthening of the GFPS and the updating of the GAD Database. The 2016 GAD Plan and Budget was already reviewed and approved by the DILG.

observations.recommendation:
Ensure that following essential elements in GAD planning and budgeting are in place, to wit:
Create or strengthen the LGU GFPS
Establish and maintain a functional GAD Database which shall aid in gender analysis for the identification of gender issues to be addressed by the agency
observations.managementAction:
AAR
CY
2014
Management ensured that charges to LDRRMF are consistent with prescribed guidelines. The Local School Board was directed to align the budget with the guidelines on the utilization of SEF. The Treasury was required to submit all lacking vouchers and the Municipal Accountant was directed to account for all lacking disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: There were still a few charges to the LDRRMF that were not related to the agency’s LDRRM activities. Priority expenses for SEF were not given priority in the current year..

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Local School Board to align their budget with the specific programs, projects and activities that strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of Republic Act No. 7160 and DECS/DBM/DILG Joint Circular no. 01series of 1998 dated April 14, 1998. This will be the basis of the management for their disbursements under SEF in order to utilize the fund for the intended beneficiaries and to prevent future suspensions and disallowance in audit.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking vouchers to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
CY
